title: torminaria clusii
intro: Torminaria clusii, also known as Tormina clusii, is a plant species in the family Apiaceae, commonly referred to as a type of wild celery or wild fennel. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its carminative, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant effects. The health benefits associated with Torminaria clusii include relief from indigestion, bloating, and respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis, as well as its potential to act as an anti-spasmodic agent. Herbal preparations of Torminaria clusii can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
